summary: Kenneth discusses individual stocks vs ETFs, comparing historical performance outcomes across various tickers (Dutch Bros, Reddit, Palantir, Intel, Altria, Walgreens, Super Micro Computer, Cava, Chipotle). He concludes that individual stock picking involves significant risk from earnings, hype, and momentum trading, and suggests some investors prefer index funds/ETFs as a lower-complexity alternative, though notes his own portfolio is mostly individual stocks.
takeaways: Individual stock trading carries extra risk from earnings surprises, momentum chasing, and stock-picking uncertainty
ETFs (SPG, FTE, SCHD) and index funds offer simpler alternatives to reduce idiosyncratic risk
Personal investing decision depends on time availability, resources, and risk tolerance rather than absolute stock vs ETF superiority
Examples show earnings can move stocks 25%+ either direction, making pre-earnings trading unpredictable
title: Should you invest in ETFs or individual stocks?? #shorts
